what_it_does: An executable acceptance test that mints build intents via surface_intent_at (and surface_build_intent), then reloads the persisted records from disk through a fresh FsStore/Suite and asserts the payload carries the ui:// address and the code:// symbols already computed by resolve_scope, while existing payload fields and bare/orphan intent behavior remain unchanged.
observations: Heavy docstring frames the test as a proof for two defects (X1 address drop, X2 symbols discard) and an additive schema fix.
Creates a temporary FsStore, discovers nodes, builds a Suite, and runs resolve_scope sanity checks against real corpus addresses.
Uses a separate FsStore + Suite on the same store root to reload surfaced records from disk.
Asserts reloaded payload contains address and symbols matching resolve_scope output.
Tests a second real address (workshop) and an orphan address (empty scope).
Verifies bare surface_build_intent persists no address/symbols keys.
Uses a global PASS counter and a custom check(label, cond) helper instead of a test framework.
standouts: The load-bearing proof is a disk reload via a fresh Suite, not an in-memory return-dict check.
Assertions are tied to a specific post-App.tsx-carve corpus state (RhmChat, suite.chat).
Validates additive-optional schema change: new keys appear when relevant, absent otherwise.
conventions: Sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN and inserts project root into sys.path.
Uses tempfile for an isolated store root.
Uses .get for optional payload keys to demonstrate reader compatibility.
Docstring includes run command and file:line references to suite.py.
concerns: Hardcoded absolute interpreter path in the docstring run command.
Assertions depend on a specific corpus carve state that could drift.
Some check assertions bundle multiple conditions, making failure messages less precise.
No use of pytest/unittest; assert failures stop the script at first failure.
notes: A regression-style acceptance test that proves the launch-context payload (ui address plus code neighbours) survives a round-trip to disk without disturbing legacy fields.
questions: What is the exact implementation of surface_intent_at and surface_build_intent in runtime/suite.py?
How is resolve_scope populated and kept in sync with the carved corpus?
What triggers the App.tsx-carve and how stable are the fixture symbols?
What is consequence_class and why is it part of the preserved payload?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches — the stated purpose is to prove the launch-context truth reaches disk, and the script does so by reloading persisted records from a fresh store handle.
intention_signals: Ensure that when an operator surfaces a build intent at a UI locus, the durable record remembers both the locus and its code neighbours so downstream tooling can reconstruct the launch context.
novelty: The disk-reload proof via a fresh Suite on the same store root, treating in-memory return values as insufficient evidence.
contribution: A concrete, runnable acceptance harness that documents the expected persisted payload shape for build intents and guards the disk-round-trip behavior of address and symbols.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test that mints build intents at UI addresses and verifies, after reloading from disk through a fresh Suite, that the persisted payload contains the address and the resolve_scope symbols while preserving existing fields.
intention_for_embedding: To prove that launch-context metadata (ui address and code neighbours) reaches durable storage without breaking legacy payload readers.

symbols.does: Lightweight assertion helper that increments a global pass counter and prints an ok line.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) asserts the supplied condition, increments the module-level PASS counter, and prints a success message; it is the only function defined in the file and drives every acceptance assertion.
